data.body:
G4 child-consistency review (parent 3fe1 "Consistent error-handling pattern" + this CLI-surface sweep child; whole sibling roster considered). One high-confidence cross-child finding (conflicting assumptions / consumer impact). Other modes clean.

Verified against source (search_files/read_file):
- In `src/rebar/_cli/` there are ZERO `print(json.dumps(...))` stdout data prints; ALL stdout data output is `sys.stdout.write(...)` (e.g. `_cli/__init__.py:139` `sys.stdout.write(_json.dumps(info, ...) + "\n")`, plus ~70 `sys.stdout.write` sites across `_cli/_llm_commands.py`, `_cli/_workflow_commands.py`). The `print(json.dumps(...))`-shaped data prints this child names live in `_commands/`.
- The specific reconciler-consumed contract the child cites — `[cli, "list", "--full"]` then `json.loads(result.stdout)` at `_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py:453/466` — is served by the read-command surface in `src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py` (the `print(json.dumps(...))` read-data prints at reads_cli.py:90,92,219,221,261,263,289,336,339,401,462), which is sibling d360's file_impact ({grounding/, _engine_support/}), NOT this child's {_cli/, _commands/}. `_cli/__init__.py` routes reads into `_engine_support`; grep for read-command data prints in `_cli`/`_commands` → 0.

---
FINDING — Conflicting assumptions / consumer impact: this child's description + AC1 claim ownership of the load-bearing reconciler `rebar list --full` stdout-JSON machine contract, but that contract's data-print site and its purity guard both live in sibling scope, not in `_cli`/`_commands`.

finding: The child opens "[t]he CLI surface is where stdout `print(json.dumps(...))` data output legitimately lives — that is a machine contract and MUST stay on stdout (the reconciler `json.loads` it)", and AC1 reads "Stdout data `print(json.dumps(...))` output in `_cli/`/`_commands/` is unchanged (contract intact)." But the reconciler-consumed `list --full` data print is in `_engine_support/reads_cli.py` (sibling d360), and the stdout-purity contract test that guards `rebar list --full` is owned by sibling 8fbd — neither is in this child's scope. So this child's framing attaches a contract (and the credit for preserving it) to packages it does not touch.
criteria: [this child description "the reconciler `json.loads` it"; this child AC1 "Stdout data `print(json.dumps(...))` output in `_cli/`/`_commands/` is unchanged (contract intact)"; parent "stdout is sacred ... the reconciler `json.loads` it at reconcile.py:472"; parent SC2 "stdout stays pure (CLI data contract ... intact)"]
location: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py:453 (`[cli, "list", "--full"]`), :466 (`json.loads(result.stdout)`); src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py (read-command `print(json.dumps(...))` data prints incl. :336) — d360 file_impact; sibling 8fbd (stdout-purity `list --full` test); this child file_impact {src/rebar/_cli/, src/rebar/_commands/}.
evidence:
  - reconcile.py:453/466 runs the ticket CLI `list --full` subprocess and `json.loads` its stdout — the exact contract the parent + this child cite.
  - The read-command data prints (`list`/`show`/`search`/`ready`/...) are in `_engine_support/reads_cli.py` (search confirmed: 11 `print(json.dumps(...))` sites there); `_cli/` has ZERO `print(json.dumps(...))` (all `sys.stdout.write`). So the reconciler-consumed read output is NOT in this child's `_cli`/`_commands` scope.
  - `_engine_support/` is in d360's file_impact, not this child's ({_cli/, _commands/}); the `list --full` purity test is 8fbd's AC.
scenarios:
  - An implementer reading this child's "keep stdout data prints / the reconciler json.loads it" looks only in `_cli`/`_commands`, preserves the WRITE-command data prints (claim/transition/delete/...), and reasonably concludes "the reconciler contract is covered here." But the reconciler-consumed `list --full` print is in d360's `_engine_support/reads_cli.py` — so neither this child nor a reader of it actually guards the cited contract; responsibility silently sits with d360 (whose own G4 flags a library/core-vs-CLI mis-classification of those same stdout prints) + 8fbd (the purity test).
  - For the SPECIFIC reconciler contract it names, this child's AC1 "contract intact" is neither implemented nor tested within its own scope.
impact: A cross-child attribution gap that can lull both an implementer and a reviewer into believing the reconciler `json.loads(list --full)` contract is preserved "here," when its data-print site (_engine_support/reads_cli.py, d360) and purity guard (8fbd) are outside this pairing's scope. The real risk to that contract sits in d360's `_engine_support` sweep. Absence cross-checked: no sibling re-states the `list --full` print as living in `_cli`/`_commands`.
checklist_item: conflicting assumptions; consumer impact
suggested_fix: Re-scope the description/AC1 to the data prints this child actually owns (the `_commands/*` write-command `print(json.dumps(...))` outputs — claim/transition/delete/session_log/show_config/fsck), and explicitly hand off the reconciler `rebar list --full` contract to its true owners: data-print site `_engine_support/reads_cli.py` (sibling d360) and purity guard sibling 8fbd's `list --full` test. Either drop the "the reconciler json.loads it" claim from THIS child or add a cross-reference noting the reconciler-consumed read contract is owned by d360/8fbd, so it is provably guarded by an owning ticket rather than implied here.

---
Modes checked and CLEAN (high-confidence, benign-reading filter):
- Implicit shared state (BLE001/T201 allowlist): benign — this child removes only its OWN packages (`_cli`/`_commands`) from the 16a1-authored, 0ec1-emptied allowlist, sequenced via blocks edges (16a1 → fd72 → 0ec1); no concurrent writer.
- Scope overlap: benign — `_cli`/`_commands` are uniquely this child's; no other sweep lists them in file_impact (8d01 = __init__/reducer/graph/_store; d360 = grounding/_engine_support; ceaa = mcp_server/llm; 0569 = rebar_reconciler; 16a1 = llm/plan_review). `_cli/__init__.py` is also in 8fbd's file_impact (entrypoint stderr handler, "no except changes"), but the edits are distinct/non-conflicting and correctly ordered (8fbd → 16a1 → fd72).
- Ordering violation: benign — sits after substrate 8fbd and after 16a1 enables BLE001/scoped-T201 (8fbd → 16a1 → fd72), and blocks 0ec1; the named logger exists before this sweep migrates `_commands/` diagnostics into it.
- Dependency gap: benign for THIS pairing — the verified roster hole `src/rebar/_io/` (7 pre-seeded `# noqa: BLE001` in import_ndjson.py, owned by no sweep) is OUTSIDE this child's `_cli`/`_commands` scope and already filed against 16a1 (allowlist author) + 0ec1 (allowlist-empty gate); not re-attributed here per the absence cross-check.
- Residual references: benign — the only drift is the shared stale cite `reconcile.py:472` (actual `json.loads(stdout)` call is reconcile.py:466), inherited from the parent/8fbd; below the high-confidence bar. The substantive mis-attribution of that contract is captured as the FINDING above.

The one finding above is the conflicting-assumptions/consumer-impact mode: this child's description/AC1 claim the reconciler `list --full` stdout contract, while that contract's print site (`_engine_support/reads_cli.py`, d360) and purity guard (8fbd) live in sibling scope, not in `_cli`/`_commands`.
